mappings.rationaleAI: Theatre as a global artistic form from ancient Greece through modern performance addresses the art of theatre (A), its nature and origins (A.1), acting (A.7), the role of other arts (A.8), diverse kinds of production (B), and the history of theatre (D).
Modern theatrical drama from 1880 covering naturalism, realism, and metatheatre addresses the art of theatre (A), kinds defined by style (B.1.e), and the history of Western theatre (D.1).
Tragedy from ancient Greek theater to contemporary literature directly addresses traditional dramatic forms or genres (B.1.a.i).
Comedy covering comic drama, pantomime, music hall, and stand-up addresses traditional dramatic forms (B.1.a.i) and popular entertainments (B.1.f.iii).
Sophocles as an Athenian tragic dramatist covering stagecraft and performance addresses acting (A.7), tragedy as a traditional genre (B.1.a.i), and the history of Western theatre (D.1).
Shakespeare as playwright and actor covering dramatic works addresses acting (A.7), traditional dramatic genres (B.1.a.i), and the history of Western theatre (D.1).
Shakespeare's comic dramatic works address traditional dramatic forms (B.1.a.i) and the history of Western theatre (D.1).
Shakespeare's tragedies address traditional dramatic forms (B.1.a.i) and the history of Western theatre (D.1).
Shaw's dramatic canon addresses traditional dramatic forms (B.1.a.i) and the history of Western theatre (D.1).
Humor covering comedy and comic amusement from theoretical perspectives addresses traditional dramatic forms including comedy (B.1.a.i).
Ritual as a fundamental human activity encompassing rites of passage connects to the origins of theatre as an art (A.1) and religious theatre (B.1.b).
Architecture covering buildings from ancient Egypt through modernism provides context for theatre buildings, stages, and auditoriums (C.1).
Design covering form, function, and cultural context addresses staging and stage design (C.2).
Fashion as visual culture and identity construction addresses costumes and makeup as elements of staging (C.2).
Aesthetics examining art, beauty, and aesthetic experience addresses functions of theatre including theatre as social and moral expression (A.2).
Greek and Latin tragedy and comedy address the history of Western theatre (D.1).
Indian cinema covering relationships with theatre, radio, and television addresses kinds defined by production media (B.1.h).
Creativity covering creative expression and cultural contexts provides foundational context for the art of theatre (A).
